content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Reviewed the additive FlowPreset::IterativeMinimal bead on feat/yrb-iterative-minimal-workflow. The iterative implementer loop in execute_iterative_plan_and_implement_stage, its git-based diff fingerprint (with intentional filesystem fallback), journal event ordering, rewind-on-missing-sidecar resume via reconstruct_iterative_state_before_iteration, exit-reason precedence (Stable > MaxRounds), cycle/stage-scoped state reset via carry_forward_iterative_state, and flow dispatch guard all check out. Config validation correctly rejects zero values and stable > max relations both at workspace and project scope, with nested iterative_minimal serialized via Option + is_empty. Fallback fingerprint's inclusion of top-level target/result paths is explicit design (covered by a dedicated test, since no .gitignore is available outside a git repo). One usability concern worth a follow-up bead: workspace-level config set now walks every project config file to revalidate iterative_minimal bounds, which couples unrelated workspace edits to the parseability of every project.toml under audit/ and live/projects/. No correctness-blocking findings.

## Amendments

1. In src/contexts/workspace_governance/config.rs, `Config::set` (line 572) unconditionally calls `validate_workspace_iterative_minimal_settings`, which walks every directory under `audit/projects` and `live/projects` and reads each project's `project.toml` via `FileSystem::read_project_config`. A single corrupt, partially-written, or transiently-unreadable project config will surface as a parse/IO error from any workspace-level `rb config set ...` invocation — including for keys entirely unrelated to iterative_minimal (e.g., `settings.default_backend`, `workflow.max_qa_iterations`). The error message points at the iterative_minimal validator path rather than the offending project, which is confusing, and a workspace operator cannot recover a broken workspace config setting without first fixing the unrelated project. Consider one of: (a) scoping the cross-project walk to only runs when the key being set lives under `workflow.iterative_minimal.*`, (b) skipping unreadable project configs with a tracing::warn! and continuing, or (c) relying on the existing runtime validation in `validate_iterative_minimal_loop_settings` (already called from the engine) instead of the eager cross-scope walk on every set.
   Rationale: The current cross-project walk is belt-and-suspenders validation (the engine re-validates at runtime anyway), but its failure mode is a non-obvious usability regression: any bad project.toml anywhere in the workspace blocks every workspace config edit, with an error message that does not name the offending project. This is reachable in normal usage (half-written configs during editing, partial checkouts, concurrent writes) and worth fixing without blocking the current bead.
